an10.73:1.1.pli: “Dasayime, bhikkhave, dhammā iṭṭhā kantā manāpā dullabhā lokasmiṁ.
an10.73:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, these ten likable, desirable, and agreeable things are rare in the world.

an10.73:1.3.pli: Bhogā iṭṭhā kantā manāpā dullabhā lokasmiṁ;

an10.73:1.4.pli: vaṇṇo iṭṭho kanto manāpo dullabho lokasmiṁ;

an10.73:1.5.pli: ārogyaṁ iṭṭhaṁ kantaṁ manāpaṁ dullabhaṁ lokasmiṁ;

an10.73:1.6.pli: sīlaṁ iṭṭhaṁ kantaṁ manāpaṁ dullabhaṁ lokasmiṁ;

an10.73:1.7.pli: brahmacariyaṁ iṭṭhaṁ kantaṁ manāpaṁ dullabhaṁ lokasmiṁ;

an10.73:1.8.pli: mittā iṭṭhā kantā manāpā dullabhā lokasmiṁ;

an10.73:1.9.pli: bāhusaccaṁ iṭṭhaṁ kantaṁ manāpaṁ dullabhaṁ lokasmiṁ;

an10.73:1.10.pli: paññā iṭṭhā kantā manāpā dullabhā lokasmiṁ;

an10.73:1.11.pli: dhammā iṭṭhā kantā manāpā dullabhā lokasmiṁ;

an10.73:1.12.pli: saggā iṭṭhā kantā manāpā dullabhā lokasmiṁ.
an10.73:1.12.eng: and heaven are likable, desirable, and agreeable things that are rare in the world.

an10.73:2.1.pli: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, dasannaṁ dhammānaṁ iṭṭhānaṁ kantānaṁ manāpānaṁ dullabhānaṁ lokasmiṁ dasa dhammā paripanthā—
an10.73:2.1.eng: Ten things are roadblocks for these ten likable, desirable, and agreeable things that are rare in the world.

an10.73:2.2.pli: ālasyaṁ anuṭṭhānaṁ bhogānaṁ paripantho, amaṇḍanā avibhūsanā vaṇṇassa paripantho, asappāyakiriyā ārogyassa paripantho, pāpamittatā sīlānaṁ paripantho, indriyaasaṁvaro brahmacariyassa paripantho, visaṁvādanā mittānaṁ paripantho, asajjhāyakiriyā bāhusaccassa paripantho, asussūsā aparipucchā paññāya paripantho, ananuyogo apaccavekkhaṇā dhammānaṁ paripantho, micchāpaṭipatti saggānaṁ paripantho.
an10.73:2.2.eng: Sloth and lack of initiative are a roadblock for wealth. Lack of adornment and decoration are a roadblock for beauty. Unsuitable activity is a roadblock for health. Bad friendship is a roadblock for ethical conduct. Lack of sense restraint is a roadblock for the spiritual life. Dishonesty is a roadblock for friends. Not rehearsing is a roadblock for learning. Not wanting to listen and ask questions are roadblocks for wisdom. Lack of commitment and reviewing are roadblocks for good qualities. Wrong practice hinders heaven.

an10.73:2.3.pli: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, dasannaṁ iṭṭhānaṁ kantānaṁ manāpānaṁ dullabhānaṁ lokasmiṁ ime dasa dhammā paripanthā.
an10.73:2.3.eng: These ten things are roadblocks for these ten likable, desirable, and agreeable things that are rare in the world.

an10.73:3.1.pli: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, dasannaṁ dhammānaṁ iṭṭhānaṁ kantānaṁ manāpānaṁ dullabhānaṁ lokasmiṁ dasa dhammā āhārā—
an10.73:3.1.eng: Ten things nourish these ten likable, desirable, and agreeable things that are rare in the world.

an10.73:3.2.pli: uṭṭhānaṁ anālasyaṁ bhogānaṁ āhāro, maṇḍanā vibhūsanā vaṇṇassa āhāro, sappāyakiriyā ārogyassa āhāro, kalyāṇamittatā sīlānaṁ āhāro, indriyasaṁvaro brahmacariyassa āhāro, avisaṁvādanā mittānaṁ āhāro, sajjhāyakiriyā bāhusaccassa āhāro, sussūsā paripucchā paññāya āhāro, anuyogo paccavekkhaṇā dhammānaṁ āhāro, sammāpaṭipatti saggānaṁ āhāro.
an10.73:3.2.eng: Application and initiative nourish wealth. Adornment and decoration nourish beauty. Suitable activity nourishes health. Good friendship nourishes ethical conduct. Sense restraint nourishes the spiritual life. Honesty nourishes friends. Rehearsing nourishes learning. Eagerness to listen and ask questions nourishes wisdom. Commitment and reviewing nourish good qualities. Right practice nourishes heaven.

an10.73:3.3.pli: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, dasannaṁ dhammānaṁ iṭṭhānaṁ kantānaṁ manāpānaṁ dullabhānaṁ lokasmiṁ ime dasa dhammā āhārā”ti.
an10.73:3.3.eng: These ten things nourish these ten likable, desirable, and agreeable things that are rare in the world.”

an10.74:1.1.pli: “Dasahi, bhikkhave, vaḍḍhīhi vaḍḍhamāno ariyasāvako ariyāya vaḍḍhiyā vaḍḍhati, sārādāyī ca hoti varādāyī kāyassa.
an10.74:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, a noble disciple who grows in ten ways grows nobly, taking on what is essential and excellent in this life.

an10.74:1.3.pli: Khettavatthūhi vaḍḍhati, dhanadhaññena vaḍḍhati, puttadārehi vaḍḍhati, dāsakammakaraporisehi vaḍḍhati, catuppadehi vaḍḍhati, saddhāya vaḍḍhati, sīlena vaḍḍhati, sutena vaḍḍhati, cāgena vaḍḍhati, paññāya vaḍḍhati—
an10.74:1.3.eng: He grows in fields and lands, money and grain, wives and children, in bondservants, workers, and staff, and in livestock. And he grows in faith, ethics, learning, generosity, and wisdom.

an10.74:1.4.pli: imehi kho, bhikkhave, dasahi vaḍḍhīhi vaḍḍhamāno ariyasāvako ariyāya vaḍḍhiyā vaḍḍhati, sārādāyī ca hoti varādāyī kāyassāti.
an10.74:1.4.eng: A noble disciple who grows in ten ways grows nobly, taking on what is essential and excellent in this life.

an10.74:2.1.eng: Someone who grows in money and grain,

an10.74:2.4.eng: by relatives and friends, and even by royals.

an10.74:3.1.eng: When someone grows in faith and ethics,

an10.74:3.2.eng: wisdom, and both generosity and learning—

an10.74:3.3.eng: a good man such as he sees clearly,

an10.74:3.4.eng: and in this very life he grows in both ways.”

an10.75:1.1.pli: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
an10.75:1.1.eng: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.

an10.75:1.2.pli: Atha kho āyasmā ānando pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena migasālāya upāsikāya nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi.
an10.75:1.2.eng: Then Venerable Ānanda robed up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, went to the home of the laywoman Migasālā, where he sat on the seat spread out.

an10.75:1.3.pli: Atha kho migasālā upāsikā yenāyasmā ānando tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho migasālā upāsikā āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ etadavoca:
an10.75:1.3.eng: Then the laywoman Migasālā went up to Ānanda, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:

an10.75:2.1.pli: “Kathaṁ kathaṁ nāmāyaṁ, bhante ānanda, bhagavatā dhammo desito aññeyyo, yatra hi nāma brahmacārī ca abrahmacārī ca ubho samasamagatikā bhavissanti abhisamparāyaṁ.
an10.75:2.1.eng: “Honorable Ānanda, how on earth are we supposed to understand the teaching taught by the Buddha, when the chaste and the unchaste are both reborn in exactly the same place in the next life?

an10.75:2.2.pli: Pitā me, bhante, purāṇo brahmacārī hoti ārācārī virato methunā gāmadhammā.
an10.75:2.2.eng: My father Purāṇa was chaste, set apart, avoiding the vulgar act of sex.

an10.75:2.3.pli: So kālaṅkato bhagavatā byākato: ‘sakadāgāmī satto tusitaṁ kāyaṁ upapanno’ti.
an10.75:2.3.eng: When he passed away the Buddha declared that, since he was a once-returner, he was reborn in the host of joyful gods.

an10.75:2.4.pli: Pitāmaho me, bhante, isidatto abrahmacārī ahosi sadārasantuṭṭho.
an10.75:2.4.eng: But my uncle Isidatta was not chaste; he lived content with his wife.

an10.75:2.5.pli: Sopi kālaṅkato bhagavatā byākato: ‘sakadāgāmī satto tusitaṁ kāyaṁ upapanno’ti.
an10.75:2.5.eng: When he passed away the Buddha also declared that, since he was a once-returner, he was reborn in the host of joyful gods.

an10.75:3.1.pli: Kathaṁ kathaṁ nāmāyaṁ, bhante ānanda, bhagavatā dhammo desito aññeyyo, yatra hi nāma brahmacārī ca abrahmacārī ca ubho samasamagatikā bhavissanti abhisamparāyan”ti?
an10.75:3.1.eng: How on earth are we supposed to understand the teaching taught by the Buddha, when the chaste and the unchaste are both reborn in exactly the same place in the next life?”

an10.75:3.2.eng: “You’re right, sister, but that’s how the Buddha declared it.”

an10.75:4.1.pli: Atha kho āyasmā ānando migasālāya upāsikāya nivesane piṇḍapātaṁ gahetvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkāmi.
an10.75:4.1.eng: Then Ānanda, after receiving almsfood at Migasālā’s home, rose from his seat and left.

an10.75:4.2.pli: Atha kho āyasmā ānando pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkanto yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā ānando bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
an10.75:4.2.eng: Then after the meal, on his return from almsround, Ānanda went to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him what had happened.

an10.75:5.1.pli: “Idhāhaṁ, bhante, pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena migasālāya upāsikāya nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdiṁ.

an10.75:5.2.pli: Atha kho, bhante, migasālā upāsikā yenāhaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā maṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho, bhante, migasālā upāsikā maṁ etadavoca:

an10.75:6.1.pli: ‘Kathaṁ kathaṁ nāmāyaṁ, bhante ānanda, bhagavatā dhammo desito aññeyyo, yatra hi nāma brahmacārī ca abrahmacārī ca ubho samasamagatikā bhavissanti abhisamparāyaṁ.

an10.75:6.2.pli: Pitā me, bhante, purāṇo brahmacārī ahosi ārācārī virato methunā gāmadhammā.

an10.75:6.3.pli: So kālaṅkato bhagavatā byākato sakadāgāmī satto tusitaṁ kāyaṁ upapannoti.

an10.75:6.4.pli: Pitāmaho me, bhante, isidatto abrahmacārī ahosi sadārasantuṭṭho.

an10.75:6.5.pli: Sopi kālaṅkato bhagavatā byākato—sakadāgāmī satto tusitaṁ kāyaṁ upapannoti.

an10.75:7.1.pli: Kathaṁ kathaṁ nāmāyaṁ, bhante ānanda, bhagavatā dhammo desito aññeyyo, yatra hi nāma brahmacārī ca abrahmacārī ca ubho samasamagatikā bhavissanti abhisamparāyan’ti?

an10.75:7.2.pli: Evaṁ vutte, ahaṁ, bhante, migasālaṁ upāsikaṁ etadavocaṁ:

an10.75:8.1.pli: “Kā cānanda, migasālā upāsikā bālā abyattā ammakā ammakapaññā, ke ca purisapuggalaparopariye ñāṇe?
an10.75:8.1.eng: “Ānanda, who is this laywoman Migasālā, a foolish incompetent aunty, with an aunty’s wit? And who is it that knows how to assess individual persons?

an10.75:9.1.pli: Dasayime, ānanda, puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
an10.75:9.1.eng: These ten individuals are found in the world.

an10.75:9.3.eng: Take a certain individual who is unethical.

an10.75:9.4.pli: Tañca cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti, yatthassa taṁ dussilyaṁ aparisesaṁ nirujjhati.
an10.75:9.4.eng: And they don’t truly understand the freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom where that unethical conduct ceases without anything left over.

an10.75:9.5.pli: Tassa savanenapi akataṁ hoti, bāhusaccenapi akataṁ hoti, diṭṭhiyāpi appaṭividdhaṁ hoti, sāmāyikampi vimuttiṁ na labhati.
an10.75:9.5.eng: And they’ve not listened or learned or penetrated theoretically or found even temporary freedom.

an10.75:9.6.pli: So kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā hānāya pareti, no visesāya;
an10.75:9.6.eng: When their body breaks up, after death, they depart for a lower place, not a higher.

an10.75:9.7.eng: They’re going to a lower place, not a higher.

an10.75:10.1.pli: Idha panānanda, ekacco puggalo dussīlo hoti.
an10.75:10.1.eng: Take a certain individual who is unethical.

an10.75:10.2.pli: Tañca cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti yatthassa taṁ dussilyaṁ aparisesaṁ nirujjhati.
an10.75:10.2.eng: But they truly understand the freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom where that unethical conduct ceases without anything left over.

an10.75:10.3.pli: Tassa savanenapi kataṁ hoti, bāhusaccenapi kataṁ hoti, diṭṭhiyāpi paṭividdhaṁ hoti, sāmāyikampi vimuttiṁ labhati.
an10.75:10.3.eng: And they have listened and learned and penetrated theoretically and found at least temporary freedom.

an10.75:10.4.pli: So kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā visesāya pareti, no hānāya;
an10.75:10.4.eng: When their body breaks up, after death, they depart for a higher place, not a lower.

an10.75:10.5.eng: They’re going to a higher place, not a lower.

an10.75:11.2.pli: ‘imassapi teva dhammā, aparassapi teva dhammā. Kasmā nesaṁ eko hīno eko paṇīto’ti?
an10.75:11.2.eng: ‘This one has just the same qualities as the other, so why is one worse and one better?’

an10.75:11.3.pli: Tañhi tesaṁ, ānanda, hoti dīgharattaṁ ahitāya dukkhāya.
an10.75:11.3.eng: This will be for their lasting harm and suffering.

an10.75:12.1.eng: In this case, the individual who is unethical,

an10.75:12.2.pli: Tañca cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti yatthassa taṁ dussilyaṁ aparisesaṁ nirujjhati.
an10.75:12.2.eng: but truly understands the freedom of heart …

an10.75:12.3.pli: Tassa savanenapi kataṁ hoti, bāhusaccenapi kataṁ hoti, diṭṭhiyāpi paṭividdhaṁ hoti, sāmāyikampi vimuttiṁ labhati.
an10.75:12.3.eng: and has listened and learned and penetrated theoretically and found at least temporary freedom

an10.75:12.4.pli: Ayaṁ, ānanda, puggalo amunā purimena puggalena abhikkantataro ca paṇītataro ca.
an10.75:12.4.eng: is better and finer than the other individual.

an10.75:12.6.eng: Because the stream of the teaching carries them along.

an10.75:12.7.eng: But who knows the difference between them except a Realized One?

an10.75:12.8.eng: So, Ānanda, don’t be judgmental about individuals.

an10.75:12.10.eng: Those who pass judgment on individuals harm themselves.

an10.75:12.11.pli: Ahaṁ vā, ānanda, puggalesu pamāṇaṁ gaṇheyyaṁ yo vā panassa mādiso.
an10.75:12.11.eng: I, or someone like me, may pass judgment on people.

an10.75:13.1.pli: Idha panānanda, ekacco puggalo sīlavā hoti.
an10.75:13.1.eng: Take a certain individual who is ethical.

an10.75:13.2.pli: Tañca cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti yatthassa taṁ sīlaṁ aparisesaṁ nirujjhati.
an10.75:13.2.eng: But they don’t truly understand the freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom where that ethical conduct ceases without anything left over.

an10.75:13.3.pli: Tassa savanenapi akataṁ hoti, bāhusaccenapi akataṁ hoti, diṭṭhiyāpi appaṭividdhaṁ hoti, sāmāyikampi vimuttiṁ na labhati.
an10.75:13.3.eng: And they’ve not listened or learned or penetrated theoretically or found even temporary freedom.

an10.75:13.4.pli: So kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā hānāya pareti, no visesāya;
an10.75:13.4.eng: When their body breaks up, after death, they depart for a lower place, not a higher.

an10.75:13.5.eng: They’re going to a lower place, not a higher.

an10.75:14.1.pli: Idha panānanda, ekacco puggalo sīlavā hoti.
an10.75:14.1.eng: Take a certain individual who is ethical.

an10.75:14.2.pli: Tañca cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti yatthassa taṁ sīlaṁ aparisesaṁ nirujjhati.
an10.75:14.2.eng: And they truly understand the freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom where that ethical conduct ceases without anything left over.

an10.75:14.3.pli: Tassa savanenapi kataṁ hoti, bāhusaccenapi kataṁ hoti, diṭṭhiyāpi paṭividdhaṁ hoti, sāmāyikampi vimuttiṁ labhati.
an10.75:14.3.eng: And they’ve listened and learned and penetrated theoretically and found at least temporary freedom.

an10.75:14.4.pli: So kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā visesāya pareti, no hānāya;
an10.75:14.4.eng: When their body breaks up, after death, they depart for a higher place, not a lower.

an10.75:14.5.eng: They’re going to a higher place, not a lower.

an10.75:15.2.pli: ahaṁ vā, ānanda, puggalesu pamāṇaṁ gaṇheyyaṁ yo vā panassa mādiso.
an10.75:15.2.eng: I, or someone like me, may pass judgment on individuals.

an10.75:16.1.pli: Idha panānanda, ekacco puggalo tibbarāgo hoti.
an10.75:16.1.eng: Take a certain individual who is very lustful.

an10.75:16.2.pli: Tañca cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti yatthassa so rāgo apariseso nirujjhati.
an10.75:16.2.eng: And they don’t truly understand the freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom where that lust ceases without anything left over.

an10.75:16.3.pli: Tassa savanenapi akataṁ hoti, bāhusaccenapi akataṁ hoti, diṭṭhiyāpi appaṭividdhaṁ hoti, sāmāyikampi vimuttiṁ na labhati.
an10.75:16.3.eng: And they’ve not listened or learned or penetrated theoretically or found even temporary freedom.

an10.75:16.4.pli: So kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā hānāya pareti, no visesāya;
an10.75:16.4.eng: When their body breaks up, after death, they depart for a lower place, not a higher.

an10.75:16.5.eng: They’re going to a lower place, not a higher.

an10.75:17.1.pli: Idha panānanda, ekacco puggalo tibbarāgo hoti.
an10.75:17.1.eng: Take a certain individual who is very lustful.

an10.75:17.2.pli: Tañca cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti yatthassa so rāgo apariseso nirujjhati.
an10.75:17.2.eng: But they truly understand the freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom where that lust ceases without anything left over.

an10.75:17.3.pli: Tassa savanenapi kataṁ hoti, bāhusaccenapi kataṁ hoti, diṭṭhiyāpi paṭividdhaṁ hoti, sāmāyikampi vimuttiṁ labhati.
an10.75:17.3.eng: And they’ve listened and learned and penetrated theoretically and found at least temporary freedom.

an10.75:17.4.pli: So kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā visesāya pareti, no hānāya;
an10.75:17.4.eng: When their body breaks up, after death, they depart for a higher place, not a lower.

an10.75:17.5.eng: They’re going to a higher place, not a lower.

an10.75:18.2.pli: ahaṁ vā, ānanda, puggalesu pamāṇaṁ gaṇheyyaṁ yo vā panassa mādiso.
an10.75:18.2.eng: I, or someone like me, may pass judgment on individuals.

an10.75:19.1.pli: Idha panānanda, ekacco puggalo kodhano hoti.
an10.75:19.1.eng: Take a certain individual who is irritable.

an10.75:19.2.pli: Tañca cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti yatthassa so kodho apariseso nirujjhati.
an10.75:19.2.eng: And they don’t truly understand the freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom where that anger ceases without anything left over.

an10.75:19.3.pli: Tassa savanenapi akataṁ hoti, bāhusaccenapi akataṁ hoti, diṭṭhiyāpi appaṭividdhaṁ hoti, sāmāyikampi vimuttiṁ na labhati.
an10.75:19.3.eng: And they’ve not listened or learned or penetrated theoretically or found even temporary freedom.

an10.75:19.4.pli: So kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā hānāya pareti, no visesāya;
an10.75:19.4.eng: When their body breaks up, after death, they depart for a lower place, not a higher.

an10.75:19.5.eng: They’re going to a lower place, not a higher.

an10.75:20.1.pli: Idha panānanda, ekacco puggalo kodhano hoti.
an10.75:20.1.eng: Take a certain individual who is irritable.

an10.75:20.2.pli: Tañca cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti yatthassa so kodho apariseso nirujjhati.
an10.75:20.2.eng: But they truly understand the freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom where that anger ceases without anything left over.

an10.75:20.3.pli: Tassa savanenapi kataṁ hoti, bāhusaccenapi kataṁ hoti, diṭṭhiyāpi paṭividdhaṁ hoti, sāmāyikampi vimuttiṁ labhati.
an10.75:20.3.eng: And they’ve listened and learned and penetrated theoretically and found at least temporary freedom.

an10.75:20.4.pli: So kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā visesāya pareti, no hānāya;
an10.75:20.4.eng: When their body breaks up, after death, they depart for a higher place, not a lower.

an10.75:20.5.eng: They’re going to a higher place, not a lower.

an10.75:21.2.pli: ahaṁ vā, ānanda, puggalesu pamāṇaṁ gaṇheyyaṁ yo vā panassa mādiso.
an10.75:21.2.eng: I, or someone like me, may pass judgment on individuals.

an10.75:22.1.pli: Idha panānanda, ekacco puggalo uddhato hoti.
an10.75:22.1.eng: Take a certain individual who is restless.

an10.75:22.2.pli: Tañca cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti yatthassa taṁ uddhaccaṁ aparisesaṁ nirujjhati.
an10.75:22.2.eng: And they don’t truly understand the freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom where that restlessness ceases without anything left over.

an10.75:22.3.pli: Tassa savanenapi akataṁ hoti, bāhusaccenapi akataṁ hoti, diṭṭhiyāpi appaṭividdhaṁ hoti, sāmāyikampi vimuttiṁ na labhati.
an10.75:22.3.eng: And they’ve not listened or learned or penetrated theoretically or found even temporary freedom.

an10.75:22.4.pli: So kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā hānāya pareti, no visesāya;
an10.75:22.4.eng: When their body breaks up, after death, they depart for a lower place, not a higher.

an10.75:22.5.eng: They’re going to a lower place, not a higher.

an10.75:23.1.pli: Idha panānanda, ekacco puggalo uddhato hoti.
an10.75:23.1.eng: Take a certain individual who is restless.

an10.75:23.2.pli: Tañca cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti yatthassa taṁ uddhaccaṁ aparisesaṁ nirujjhati.
an10.75:23.2.eng: But they truly understand the freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom where that restlessness ceases without anything left over.

an10.75:23.3.pli: Tassa savanenapi kataṁ hoti, bāhusaccenapi kataṁ hoti, diṭṭhiyāpi paṭividdhaṁ hoti, sāmāyikampi vimuttiṁ labhati.
an10.75:23.3.eng: And they’ve listened and learned and penetrated theoretically and found at least temporary freedom.

an10.75:23.4.pli: So kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā visesāya pareti, no hānāya;
an10.75:23.4.eng: When their body breaks up, after death, they depart for a higher place, not a lower.

an10.75:23.5.eng: They’re going to a higher place, not a lower.

an10.75:24.2.pli: ‘imassapi teva dhammā, aparassapi teva dhammā. Kasmā nesaṁ eko hīno eko paṇīto’ti?
an10.75:24.2.eng: ‘This one has just the same qualities as the other, so why is one worse and one better?’

an10.75:24.3.pli: Tañhi tesaṁ, ānanda, hoti dīgharattaṁ ahitāya dukkhāya.
an10.75:24.3.eng: This will be for their lasting harm and suffering.

an10.75:25.1.pli: Tatrānanda, yvāyaṁ puggalo uddhato hoti tañca cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti yatthassa taṁ uddhaccaṁ aparisesaṁ nirujjhati,
an10.75:25.1.eng: In this case the individual who is restless, but truly understands the freedom of heart …

an10.75:25.2.pli: tassa savanenapi kataṁ hoti, bāhusaccenapi kataṁ hoti, diṭṭhiyāpi paṭividdhaṁ hoti, sāmāyikampi vimuttiṁ labhati.
an10.75:25.2.eng: and has listened and learned and penetrated theoretically and found at least temporary freedom

an10.75:25.3.pli: Ayaṁ, ānanda, puggalo amunā purimena puggalena abhikkantataro ca paṇītataro ca.
an10.75:25.3.eng: is better and finer than the other individual.

an10.75:25.5.eng: Because the stream of the teaching carries them along.

an10.75:25.6.eng: But who knows the difference between them except a Realized One?

an10.75:25.7.eng: So, Ānanda, don’t be judgmental about individuals.

an10.75:25.9.eng: Those who pass judgment on individuals harm themselves.

an10.75:25.10.pli: Ahaṁ vā, ānanda, puggalesu pamāṇaṁ gaṇheyyaṁ yo vā panassa mādiso.
an10.75:25.10.eng: I, or someone like me, may pass judgment on individuals.

an10.75:26.1.pli: Kā cānanda, migasālā upāsikā bālā abyattā ammakā ammakapaññā, ke ca purisapuggalaparopariye ñāṇe.
an10.75:26.1.eng: Who is this laywoman Migasālā, a foolish incompetent aunty, with an aunty’s wit? And who is it that knows how to assess individual persons?

an10.75:26.2.pli: Ime kho, ānanda, dasa puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
an10.75:26.2.eng: These ten individuals are found in the world.

an10.75:27.1.pli: Yathārūpena, ānanda, sīlena purāṇo samannāgato ahosi tathārūpena sīlena isidatto samannāgato abhavissa, nayidha purāṇo isidattassa gatimpi aññassa.
an10.75:27.1.eng: If Isidatta had achieved Purāṇa’s level of ethical conduct, Purāṇa could not have even known Isidatta’s destination.

an10.75:27.2.pli: Yathārūpāya cānanda, paññāya isidatto samannāgato ahosi tathārūpāya paññāya purāṇo samannāgato abhavissa, nayidha isidatto purāṇassa gatimpi aññassa.
an10.75:27.2.eng: And if Purāṇa had achieved Isidatta’s level of wisdom, Isidatta could not have even known Purāṇa’s destination.

an10.75:27.3.pli: Iti kho, ānanda, ime puggalā ubho ekaṅgahīnā”ti.
an10.75:27.3.eng: So both individuals were lacking in one respect.”
